heading: Notice and wait requirements for certain projects
label: 18233a Notice and wait requirements for certain projects
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (a). , substituted “in excess of the amount specified in ” for “in an amount in excess of $750,000” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–92, § 2801(1)section 2805(b)(1) of this titleSubsec. (b)(3). , substituted “subsection (e) of ) that costs less than the amount specified in subsection (d) of such section” for “) that costs less than $7,500,000”.Pub. L. 114–92, § 2801(2)section 2811 of this titlesection 2811(e) of this title2011—Subsec. (b)(2)(B)(ii). substituted “section 2805(a)” for “section 2805(a)(2)”.Pub. L. 112–812004— amended section generally, substituting provisions relating to notice and wait requirements for certain projects for provisions relating to limitation on certain projects and authority to carry out small projects with operation and maintenance funds.Pub. (b)(2). , title X, § 1087(a)(22)(B)], substituted “section 2805(c)(1)(B)” for “section 2805(c)(2)”.Pub. L. 106–398, § 1 [[div. A]1999—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “and the Committee on Armed Services” for “and the Committee on National Security”.Pub. L. 106–65, § 1067(1)Subsec. (a)(2)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 106–65, § 2806(a)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Under such regulations as the Secretary of Defense may prescribe, a project authorized under that costs $500,000 or less may be carried out with funds available for operations and maintenance.”Pub. L. 106–65, § 2806(b)section 18233(a) of this title1996—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “$1,500,000” for “$400,000”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 2801(c)substituted “the Committee on Armed Services and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on National Security and the Committee on Appropriations of the” for “the Committees on Armed Services and on Appropriations of the Senate and”.Pub. L. 104–106Subsec. (b). , substituted “$500,000” for “$300,000”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 2801(b)1994—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1664(b)(2)section 2233a of this titleSubsec. (a)(1). , substituted “18233” for “2233”.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1664(b)(5)(A)Subsec. (b). , substituted “18233(a)” for “2233(a)”.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1664(b)(5)(B)1991—Subsec. (b). substituted “$300,000” for “$200,000”.Pub. L. 102–1901987—Subsec. (a)(2)(B)(ii)(II). substituted “specified by ” for “specified by law”.Pub. L. 100–26section 2805(a)(2) of this titleSubsec. (b). substituted “$200,000” for “$100,000”.Pub. L. 100–1801984—Subsec. (b). substituted “$100,000” for “$50,000”.Pub. L. 98–4071983—Subsec. (a)(1). substituted “$400,000” for “$200,000”.Pub. L. 98–1151982— substituted “Limitation on certain projects; authority to carry out small projects with operation and maintenance funds” for “Limitation” as section catchline and completely revised text. Before such revision section had provided that no expenditure or contribution of more than $175,000 could be made under for any facility until after the expiration of thirty days from the date upon which the Secretary of Defense or his designee notified the Senate and the House of Representatives of the location, nature, and estimated cost of such facility, but that such requirement did not apply to facilities acquired by lease, facilities acquired, constructed, expanded, rehabilitated, converted, or equipped to restore or replace facilities damaged or destroyed, where the Senate and the House of Representatives had been notified of that action, and that, under such regulations as the Secretary of Defense might prescribe, any project authorized pursuant to section 2233(a) which did not cost more than $50,000 could be accomplished from appropriations available for maintenance and operations.Pub. L. 97–214section 2233 of this title1979—Par. (1). substituted “$175,000” for “$100,000”.Pub. L. 96–1251975—Par. (2). substituted “$50,000” for “$25,000”.Pub. L. 94–1071974—Par. (1). substituted “$100,000” for “$850,000”.Pub. L. 93–5521962— designated existing provisions as par. (1), substituted “until after the expiration of thirty days from the date upon which the Secretary of Defense or his designee notifies the Senate and the House of Representatives of the location, nature, and estimated cost of such facility” for “that has not been authorized by a law authorizing appropriations for specific facilities for reserve forces”, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 87–554

children.text: the Secretary of Defense has notified the congressional defense committees of the location, nature, and estimated cost of the facility; and

a period of 21 days has elapsed after the notification has been received by those committees or, if over sooner, a period of 14 days has elapsed after the date on which a copy of the notification is provided in an electronic medium pursuant to .section 480 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the scope of work of the project, as approved by Congress, is not proposed to be reduced by more than 25 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 200 percent of the amount specified by as the maximum amount for a minor military construction project.section 2805(a) of this title
